name: OpenRPC MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server by OpenRPC maintainer shanejonas that enables AI agents to interact with OpenRPC-described JSON-RPC APIs — including Ethereum and other blockchain node APIs. Parses OpenRPC specification documents and generates dynamic MCP tools from the spec, enabling agents to call any JSON-RPC method defined in an OpenRPC document.
use_cases: Calling Ethereum and EVM JSON-RPC methods from blockchain agent workflows
Dynamically generating MCP tools from OpenRPC specification documents
Interacting with any JSON-RPC API that has an OpenRPC spec from integration agents
Testing and exploring JSON-RPC APIs from development agents
not_for: REST API integration (OpenRPC is JSON-RPC specific, not REST)
Simple HTTP API calls (use fetch/HTTP MCPs for REST APIs)
best_when: An agent needs to interact with Ethereum nodes, blockchain RPC endpoints, or other JSON-RPC services that have OpenRPC specifications — dynamically generating tools from the spec rather than hardcoding API calls.
avoid_when: Your target API is REST (not JSON-RPC), or doesn't have an OpenRPC specification document. The OpenRPC ecosystem is primarily used in blockchain/Ethereum contexts.

auth.notes: Authentication depends on the JSON-RPC endpoint being used. Ethereum nodes may require API keys (Infura, Alchemy). Configure endpoint URL and auth per JSON-RPC provider.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free and open source; JSON-RPC endpoint costs vary by provider
pricing.paid_tiers: None for MCP server; Ethereum node providers (Infura, Alchemy) have paid tiers
pricing.notes: Free open source from OpenRPC project maintainer shanejonas. Underlying JSON-RPC endpoint costs vary (public Ethereum nodes are free, Infura/Alchemy have paid tiers).

performance.rate_limits: Depends on JSON-RPC endpoint rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: OpenRPC is primarily used in blockchain/Ethereum ecosystems — limited use outside that context
Dynamic tool generation from OpenRPC spec may produce many tools — agents need to select correctly
Blockchain write transactions via JSON-RPC are irreversible — require explicit user confirmation
By the OpenRPC project maintainer — higher quality than average community MCP
agent_readiness.security_notes: Write operations on blockchain are irreversible — never execute transaction-signing methods autonomously. Private keys must never be passed through MCP tool parameters. Use read-only methods autonomously; gate write methods behind explicit confirmation.

security_score_components.security_notes: Blockchain transactions are irreversible. Never pass private keys through MCP. Read-only RPC calls are safe; write operations need explicit authorization. HTTPS to JSON-RPC endpoints.
